description: Fix NaN retryAfterMs bug in Slack plugin rate-limit handling — when HTTP 429 has no Retry-After header, parseInt('unknown') produces NaN which is passed as retryAfterMs to ToolError.rateLimited(), making programmatic retry decisions impossible for AI agents.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/slack && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix NaN retryAfterMs on HTTP 429 rate-limit responses
userStories.description: As an AI agent calling Slack tools, I want the retryAfterMs field in rate-limit errors to be a valid number or undefined so that I can make informed retry decisions.
acceptanceCriteria: When HTTP 429 has a valid numeric Retry-After header (e.g., '30'), retryAfterMs is the value in milliseconds (30000)
When HTTP 429 has no Retry-After header, retryAfterMs is undefined (not NaN)
When HTTP 429 has a non-numeric Retry-After header, retryAfterMs is undefined (not NaN)
The error message includes Retry-After information when the header is present
The error message does not include 'unknown' as a retry value when the header is absent
cd plugins/slack && npm run build passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run format:check passes
